next_steps: Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with cell count, Gram stain, fungal/bacterial/viral cultures, and parasitic microscopic examination for larvae
Perform skin biopsy of purpuric rash for histopathology and tissue culture
Order blood and sputum cultures for slow-growing pathogens
Review immunosuppressive regimen and consider reduction of calcineurin inhibitor and antiproliferative agents
Obtain abdominal CT scan to evaluate for mesenteric ischemia or perforated viscus given the severe ileus
Initiate empiric treatment with ivermectin for suspected Strongyloides hyperinfection
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with cell count, Gram stain, and parasitic microscopic examination for larvae
Perform punch biopsy of purpuric rash for histopathology and tissue culture
Obtain abdominal CT scan with contrast to evaluate for bowel ischemia, perforation, or vasculitis
Stool examination for ova and parasites (specifically looking for rhabditiform larvae)
Serum cortisol and ACTH testing to assess for adrenal insufficiency
Assess peripheral blood smear for circulating parasites or atypical cells
